# Temporal-pyramid L1/L2 durable aggregation lane + velocity fields

## Context

Leaf B of Epic #12679's implementation decomposition (map: https://github.com/neomjs/neo/issues/12679#issuecomment-4862079789). Filed pre-`Accepted` per ADR 0005 Â§2.3 for planning visibility. Sequenced **after Leaf A** (`#14433` â€” the storage substrate this lane writes into).

**â›” Merge gate:** merge-blocked until ADR 0028 is `Accepted` (PR `#14428`), per `#11376` Â§6 and ADR 0028 Â§5.

## The Problem

The durable tiers do not exist: no lane aggregates sessions (L1) and days (L2) into the `temporal-summary` collection, and the deterministic velocity fields (the substrate's answer to the "150+ PRs a week" V-B-A failure class) have no producer.

## The Architectural Reality

Bound by ADR 0028 (re-verify `Accepted` at claim):

- **Â§2.1:** the aggregation lane reuses the **landed** `#12676` pattern â€” `MaintenanceBackpressureService` heavy-maintenance lane + supervised-child scheduled task + most-recent-first bounded batches â€” scheduled under ADR 0022 fairness (must not starve REM/defrag siblings).
- **Â§2.4:** L1/L2 records carry the deterministic velocity fields (`mergedPrs`, `devCommits`, `sessionsPerAgent`, `highImpactSessions`, `adrsLanded`, `sandboxesGraduated`), each bound to its named source substrate at implementation; **prose is never the source of truth**. Velocity fields are folded into this leaf (not separate) because they ship with the aggregation write path â€” a separate metrics leaf would re-open the same files (fold rationale in the map).
- **Â§2.6:** per-agent partitions + one unified track with attribution.
- **Â§2.3 boundary:** writes go through the Leaf-A schema (`SUMMARY_DAILY` labels via this deterministic lane only â€” never extractor-prompt widening).
- Retention/versioning policy: owned here, within the `version` metadata field.
- New `.mjs` placement via `structural-pre-flight` at claim (likely `ai/daemons/` scheduled-task + `ai/services/` territory; contract stays directory-agnostic).

## The Fix

One PR: the L1 (per-session) + L2 (daily) aggregation lane writing collection records + `SUMMARY_DAILY` labels, velocity fields populated from named sources, retention/versioning policy, scheduled under the backpressure/fairness substrate. No L3â€“L5 logic (Leaf C), no consumer surface.

## Contract Ledger Matrix

| Target Surface | Source of Authority | Proposed Behavior | Fallback | Docs | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Aggregation lane (new scheduled task + service logic) | ADR 0028 Â§2.1 + ADR 0022 + landed `#12676` pattern | Bounded, most-recent-first, backpressure-leased L1/L2 writes | No durable tiers (status quo) | ADR 0028 Â§2.1 | Task registered + lease behavior tested |
| L1/L2 records in `temporal-summary` | ADR 0028 Â§2.3/Â§2.4/Â§2.6 (via Leaf A schema) | Five-field metadata + velocity fields + partition keys | â€” | ADR 0028 Â§2.4 table | Field-source binding asserted per field |
| Retention/versioning | ADR 0028 Â§4 (negative/handoffs) | Policy defined within `version`; bounded growth | Unbounded growth (rejected) | This ticket â†’ impl docs | Policy tests |

## Decision Record impact

`depends-on ADR 0028` (Accepted-gate) Â· `aligned-with ADR 0022` (scheduling fairness) Â· consumes Leaf A (`#14433`) schema.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] L1 + L2 aggregation runs as a supervised, backpressure-leased scheduled lane (most-recent-first, bounded batches).
- [ ] Every Â§2.4 velocity field populated from its named source; per-field source binding documented; no prose-derived values.
- [ ] Per-agent + unified partition tracks written per Â§2.6.
- [ ] `SUMMARY_DAILY` labels written only by this lane (extractor untouched).
- [ ] Retention/versioning policy defined + tested within the `version` field.
- [ ] PR held un-merge-ready until ADR 0028 `Accepted`; gate stated in the PR body.
- [ ] Cross-family review before merge.

## Out of Scope

- L3â€“L5 synthesis (Leaf C). Consumer surfaces. Schema creation (Leaf A). Backfill of deep history (a follow-up policy decision once the lane is proven forward-running).

## Avoided Traps

- **Durable compression above L2** â€” rejected (ADR 0028 anti-anchor; photocopy cascade).
- **A universal PR impact score** â€” rejected (Â§2.5; named sources only).
- **Separate velocity-metrics leaf** â€” rejected (fold rationale above).
- **New cron per tierĂ—partition** â€” rejected (the L3â€“L5-dynamic shape exists precisely to avoid it).

## AC addendum (owner) â€” L2 notable-events layer (operator-seeded candidate 3, sited HERE: Leaf B owns L2 ingestion)

**AC (additive):**
- [ ] L2 daily facts carry a **bounded `notableEvents` list**: per entry, an artifact ref (PR/issue/discussion/session id) + a one-line what/why â€” the reasoning layer REFERENCED, never copied (full conversation bodies stay in their source substrates; L2 remains the deterministic single-writer aggregation lane, no extractor-prompt changes).
- [ ] Boundedness is a contract: per-window entry cap declared in the schema; overflow degrades to counts+refs, never to unbounded prose.
- [ ] The answer to "WHAT happened" is testable: the L3â€“L5 synthesis (see #14435's window-parameterized AC) consumes `notableEvents` â€” a rendered digest for a window with notable activity must cite â‰Ą1 entry, not counts alone.
- [ ] Standing constraint carried: wherever digests feed generation or ranking contexts, the #14548 OQ8 generator-firewall applies (human/decision surfaces by default; input-manifest declarations where agents consume).

Provenance: operator peer-seed 2026-07-04 (his question was "WHAT happened," which counts alone can't answer), relayed + V-B-A'd by Vega (A2A cc40197a), owner-disposed on #14433 (issuecomment-4880222160). Body-fold rides my next substantive pass; addendum = durable record (house pattern).
